cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: Agent Visibility Is Model-Dependent: Brand Scores Vary by AI Model Used
stories.plain: A new framework argues that brand visibility in agentic commerce is not a fixed score — it shifts depending on which AI model an agent uses and when. This matters because marketers optimizing for one model may be invisible to another, making model-aware GEO strategy essential for brands competing in AI-driven discovery.

stories.head: 156 AI & Technology Statistics Marketers Should Know in 2026
stories.plain: A fully sourced roundup of 156 statistics covering AI adoption, agentic AI, ROI, governance, and workforce impact in 2026. Useful as a benchmark reference for teams making the case internally for AI investment or building governance frameworks — though it is a compiled digest rather than original research.

stories.head: Tec-Do Launches Navos 2.0 Multi-Agent Marketing System at WAIC
stories.plain: Tec-Do unveiled Navos 2.0 at the World AI Conference — a multi-agent marketing system that plans and executes campaigns across office productivity tools. This signals a shift toward agentic platforms that automate end-to-end marketing workflows rather than just generating content, which changes how martech stacks are evaluated and bought.

stories.head: Brand Visibility Framework Highlights Trust and Data Resolvability for AI Agents
stories.plain: The same framework introduces the concept of a brand's data surface — the information an AI agent can resolve, compare, and trust about a brand. Marketers must now think about structured, trustworthy data availability as a prerequisite for being chosen by autonomous agents, not just by human searchers.

stories.head: 2026 Martech Stats: Governance and Workforce Data Now Central to AI Planning
stories.plain: Within the 156-statistic digest, the governance and workforce sections stand out as newly prominent categories alongside traditional adoption metrics. This reflects growing pressure on marketing teams to justify AI decisions to regulators and leadership, making these figures directly useful for budget and policy conversations.

stories.head: Salesforce's Agentforce struggles expose marketing's agentic AI readiness gap
stories.plain: Slow adoption of Salesforce Agentforce reveals that poor data quality and lack of operational readiness — not the AI itself — are the real blockers for enterprise agentic marketing. This matters because it reframes the buying conversation: tools aren't the bottleneck, internal foundations are, and that changes where budget and effort should go first.

stories.head: 91% of marketers claim an AI search strategy but most are far behind on execution
stories.plain: A survey of 600+ marketing and PR professionals finds a stark gap between confidence in AI visibility strategy and actual execution, with 62% feeling already behind competitors. This is a concrete data point on how quickly GEO has become a strategic priority — and how unprepared most teams still are to act on it.

stories.head: 5 questions to ask AI vendors before you buy anything
stories.plain: A practical buying guide that cuts through AI hype by focusing on business value proof, data policies, and implementation realities before signing a contract. Directly useful for anyone in a procurement or evaluation role, and reflects a maturing market where buyers are becoming more demanding and vendors must demonstrate real outcomes.

stories.head: Looking beyond agentic AI hype: why enterprise-wide ROI remains elusive
stories.plain: An honest assessment finding that most agentic AI deployments are siloed by function or business unit with no known examples of enterprise-wide success. This signals that the technology is still in a constrained early phase, which directly affects how buyers should set expectations and structure pilots to avoid wasted investment.

stories.head: Agent visibility scores are model-dependent, not just data-dependent
stories.plain: Argues that brand visibility frameworks for agentic commerce treat the AI agent as a fixed variable when it is actually constantly changing. A brand's discoverability score shifts with every model update, meaning point-in-time audits can mislead strategy. This is a fundamental challenge for anyone building a GEO or agentic brand presence program.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: Agentic AI's real-world friction is exposing the gap between hype and ROI
body: Salesforce's public struggles with agentic AI are not an isolated vendor story — they reflect a systemic mismatch between how autonomous agents are sold and how they actually behave inside marketing workflows. Agents fail unpredictably, and the failure modes are often invisible to the teams deploying them.

Analysts and practitioners are now pushing past the launch-day demos to ask harder ROI questions. The advice converging across sources is to start with tightly scoped, measurable tasks rather than broad automation promises — because diffuse deployments produce diffuse accountability & diffuse results.
ev: Agile Brand · Beyond agentic hype & real ROI

themes.head: Content as commodity: differentiation has moved upstream
body: When any brand can generate polished copy, images, and video at near-zero cost, the content artifact itself stops being the moat. The new competitive surface is the proprietary data, lived customer relationships, and editorial judgment that sit behind the content — things AI tools cannot replicate from a prompt.

This shifts strategy decisions: budget and talent should flow toward first-party data collection, distinctive brand voice governance, and human CX moments rather than toward volume production. The marketers who treat AI as a production shortcut without investing in those upstream assets are building on sand.
ev: MarTech · Content no longer competitive advantage
Agile Brand · Human edge in automated CX
MarTech · SaaS can't compete on software alone

themes.head: AI search strategy is widely claimed, rarely real
body: New research finds 91% of marketers say they have an AI search strategy, yet most are doing little more than sprinkling keywords into existing content. Agent visibility is also model-dependent — a brand that surfaces well in one LLM's answers may be invisible in another's, a dynamic most teams have not yet mapped.

The practical decision this forces: treat AI search as a distinct channel requiring its own content architecture, structured data, and citation-building strategy — not a bolt-on to legacy SEO. Teams that conflate the two will underperform in an environment where AI-mediated discovery is already the default for millions of queries.
ev: MarTech Series · 91% claim AI search strategy
Agile Brand · Agent visibility is model-dependent
MarTech · Look past AI to see where martech is going

themes.head: Data infrastructure debt is the real blocker to AI-powered marketing
body: Across the month, a consistent sub-theme emerged: AI ambitions routinely collide with fragmented, poorly governed data stacks. The 'enterprise Frankenstack' — dozens of loosely connected point solutions — means that even well-funded teams cannot feed AI tools clean, timely, unified data.

Vendors like Creatio are explicitly positioning against this complexity, and the analyst conversation around paid media ROI points to the same root cause — underinvestment in measurement & data infrastructure, not in media spend itself. Until data debt is addressed, AI layers on top produce unreliable outputs and erode trust in the entire stack.

themes.head: The talent & skill vacuum AI adoption is quietly creating
body: With AI absorbing the repetitive execution tasks that used to train junior marketers, organizations face a structural problem: the apprenticeship pathway into senior judgment is eroding faster than new training models are being built. By the time teams realize the skill gap, the experienced practitioners who could close it will have moved on.

This is not a distant HR problem — it surfaces now in AI vendor evaluation, where teams lack the domain expertise to interrogate model outputs or ask the right due-diligence questions before signing contracts. Investing in structured human skill development alongside AI adoption is becoming a strategic necessity, not an optional culture initiative.
ev: MarTech · 5 questions to ask AI vendors
MarTech · AI adoption hits 90% as CX paths diverge

month.takeaway: The month's news converges on a single uncomfortable truth: AI adoption is outpacing the data quality, strategic clarity, and human expertise needed to make it work — and the gap is widening fastest where the hype is loudest.
